calendar.matters: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Special Motion To Strike; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sharon He; Declaration Of Kyle Kelly; And Exhibits

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Continued To Aug-26-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Confirming Discovery Is Not Stayed, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Confirming Discovery Is Not Stayed, And For Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Tibor Szabo, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Sharon He and Kyle Kelly, in propria persona, Defendants.

The matter is not argued/contested. The matter is discussed. At the conclusion of discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Confirming Discovery Is Not Stayed, And For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED.

Defendants' notice of appeal from the order denying their special motion to strike under the anti-SLAPP statute "stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby, including enforcement of the judgment or order, but the trial court may proceed upon any other matter embraced in the action and not affected by the judgment or order." (Code of Civil Procedure section 916(a).) Where a party appeals an order on an anti-SLAPP motion, the appeal stays proceedings on the merits of the claims embraced by the anti-SLAPP motion. (Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (35 Cal.4th 180, 191.) Here, Plaintiff's discovery appears to seek evidence related to the claims embraced by Defendants' anti-SLAPP motion. Defendants' appeal of the order denying that motion triggered a new stay of proceedings on those claims, including discovery proceedings. Thus, at this time the court is without authority to compel responses to Plaintiff's discovery requests.

The Court of Appeal's 5/19/2026 Default Notice has no effect on the court's reasoning or order.

Notably, at unnumbered page 2 of their Opposition Brief, Defendants purport to quote Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(g) to read: "All discovery proceedings in the action shall be stayed upon the filing of a notice of appeal from an order granting or denying a special motion to strike until the appeal has been finally determined." Section 425.16(g) says nothing of the sort. Section 425.16(g), in fact, reads: "All discovery proceedings in the action shall be stayed upon the filing of a notice of motion made pursuant to this section. The stay of discovery shall remain in effect until notice of entry of the order ruling on the motion. The court, on noticed motion and for good cause shown, may order that specified discovery be conducted notwithstanding this subdivision." Of course, the actual text does not help Defendants cause as much as the imagined text. Defendants are admonished to accurately represent authorities and confirm that authorities are, in fact, as they represent them to be.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, an order is executed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Limited Discovery
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's Motion For Leave To Conduct Limited Discovery is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff moves for leave to conduct limited discovery under Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(g), which applies when a special motion to strike under the anti-SLAPP statute is pending. The court here has already adjudicated the only pending special motion to strike. Thus, this motion is unnecessary and, on those grounds, ordered off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion: Special Motion To Strike; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Kyle Kelly; Declaration Of Sharon He; Request For Judicial Notice; Exhibits
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Tibor Szabo 415-424-2981 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Sharon He 408-529-5168 Defendant in Pro Per.
Kyle Kelly 415-342-4790 Defendant in Pro Per.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Ms. He contests and presents arguments on factual errors and legal errors.
Mr. Szabo responds; failed to meet critera, discvoery being evaded, have an appeal, completely compliant with rules, emails to the public, fraud and malice, requests to uphold the TR.
The Court comments and poses questions.
Ms. He presents a brief rebuttal; public issues, fire dept. issue, building not up to code.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Sharon He and Kyle Kelly's special motion to strike Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's complaint under the anti-SLAPP statute.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***
